title: BOOT0 and BL31 provenance/package audit
question: Do preserved baseline artifacts or local Strix sources expose BOOT0/BL31/TF-A code or binaries with an SDMMC0 firewall/security handoff bit?
findings: Preserved metadata confirms the working boot chain is documented as BOOT0 -> ATF/BL31 -> U-Boot 2018.07 -> extlinux -> Linux, but BOOT0 and BL31 provenance remain unresolved.
Installed/package metadata found u-boot-aw2501 2018.07-17, u-boot-radxa-a733 2018.07-17, and u-boot-menu 4.2.2.
The preserved baseline explicitly reports no packages matching *boot0*, *bl31*, or *trusted*.
Local Strix searches did not find an A733/sun60i BOOT0, BL31, TF-A, or trusted-firmware source tree that can be audited for SDMMC0 handoff bits.
No local binary/package evidence names an SDMMC0 firewall, NSI, IOMMU, secure-world, or storage-master configuration step.
decision: No kernel patch. The boot-chain provenance gap is real, but current local evidence does not expose a Linux-applicable SDMMC0 IDMAC handoff bit.
next_responsible_step: Keep the maintainer/vendor question open; only revisit BOOT0/BL31 if a source package, source repository, or read-only extracted boot binary inventory becomes available.
